title: MacOS 26.2 - Running MSSQL 2025 using Rosetta result in crash related to AVX instructions
body:
### Issue Description

When trying to run MSSQL 2025 via Podman on MAC it crashes with an AVX instruction issue.

It worked earlier with MSSQL 2022.

Rosetta is enabled on the podman virtual machine.

$ podman version: `podman version 5.8.0`

[hardware-info.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25719934/hardware-info.txt)

[podman-info.json](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25719935/podman-info.json)

[sw_vers.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25719938/sw_vers.txt)

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. podman run -e 'ACCEPT_EULA=Y' -e 'SA_PASSWORD=Str0ng!Passw0rd' -p 1433:1433 --platform linux/amd64 mcr.microsoft.com/mssql/server:2025-latest
2. Container crashes


### Describe the results you received

Output from the podman run command:

```
SQL Server 2025 will run as non-root by default.
  This container is running as user mssql.
  To learn more visit https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?linkid=2099216.
  assertion failed [x86_avx_state_ptr->xsave_header.xfeatures == kSupportedXFeatureBits]:
  (ThreadContextSignals.cpp:414 rt_sigreturn)
```

### Describe the results you expected

The MSSQL container running :)

comments.body: This is not Podman exclusive. It is Rosetta limitation. This was reported in mssql-docker repo https://github.com/microsoft/mssql-docker/issues/802#issuecomment-3363509836 and there are reports of version 2025 not running using Docker Desktop as well. It has to be fixed either by Microsoft (doing more available instruction checks) or Apple (improving compatibility in the Rosetta layer).

@arixmkii we are seeing that for both OrbStack and Docker the MSSQL 2025 image works as intended. 
The issue is only present on Podman.

So I believe there something that podman is able to do.

According to this issue: https://github.com/microsoft/mssql-docker/issues/942
Microsoft has already pushed changes that makes it work.

@NicolaiOksen thank you for the updates! I'm not a docker user and was only following the issue about native arm support (I use krun provider, which doesn't support Rosetta) of mssql and saw people complaining there. It is great that people now have options to run mssql latest on macOS.

I tried that (just copied and pasted the command) with an older version of Podman (Client: `v5.7.0`, Machine: `v5.5.2`), and it seems to work. I am still running an older version of macOS. Also, for newer versions of Podman (v5.6+), you need to enable Rosetta. Here is more info:  https://blog.podman.io/2025/08/podman-5-6-released-rosetta-status-update/

I plan to upgrade macOS today, so I will try to reproduce the issue on the latest macOS with the latest Podman. I will get back to you once I verify it. In the meantime, you can also try verifying it yourself.

`mcr.microsoft.com/mssql/server:2025-latest` digest: `sha256:5ffdffd2ba852051c2fc2f5b310391bf896dbaf8d16cc026a3c859d6214b30d1`

> Machine: v5.5.2

This could be why it worked. My hunch is that it is something with the latest Fedora CoreOS kernel, which doesn't isolate something unsupported from Rosetta, where Docker and OrbStack have a patch in their kernels to workaround this issue. 

I managed to reproduce the issue with applehv provider, but my macOS is missing latest 26.3 update (details: it is 26.2 running on M4 Pro chip, client and machine 5.8.0).

> > Machine: v5.5.2
> 
> This could be why it worked. My hunch is that it is something with the latest Fedora CoreOS kernel, which doesn't isolate something unsupported from Rosetta, where Docker and OrbStack have a patch in their kernels to workaround this issue.
> 
Yep, this is possible! Just check if you've enabled Rosetta in your Podman machine.

> Just check if you've enabled Rosetta in your Podman machine.

Rosetta is operational on this machine, because I'm able to run 2022 version of sql server. It will not run w/o Rosetta.

> 
> Yep, this is possible! Just check if you've enabled Rosetta in your Podman machine.

I've also verified that Rosetta is enabled. Like @arixmkii I've been running SQL Server 2022 just fine.
It is only 2025 that isn't working.

I was able to reproduce the issue. I believe this is a bug on the SQL Server 2025 side, and there is not much we can do to address it here. The only workaround I can suggest, though it isn't ideal, is to create a Podman machine using an older image with kernel `6.12`.

You could use: `podman machine init --image docker://quay.io/podman/machine-os:5.5 test3`.

However, this would effectively downgrade your Podman version to `5.5`, which is not recommended. Since there is no further action we can take, I would close this issue. WDYT? @Luap99

> I was able to reproduce the issue. I believe this is a bug on the SQL Server 2025 side, and there is not much we can do to address it here. The only workaround I can suggest, though it isn't ideal, is to create a Podman machine using an older image with kernel `6.12`.
> 
> You could use: `podman machine init --image docker://quay.io/podman/machine-os:5.5 test3`.
> 
> However, this would effectively downgrade your Podman version to `5.5`, which is not recommended. Since there is no further action we can take, I would close this issue. WDYT? [@Luap99](https://github.com/Luap99)

I'm seeing the same issue, even when downgrading to the `5.5` image.

Since it is working on Docker + OrbStack, I'm having a hard time seeing how this can be a Microsoft issue?

We have no control over what the emulation does, it is using either qemu-user-static or rosetta and we have no way of supporting them directly other than just shipping them as is in the machine image. The same applies to the kernel which is just the default fedora kernel.

If there are ways to patch the emulation and/or kernel than these fixes must be upstreamed so they get included in the normal packages we include. We as a team really have no capacity to build an entirely custom OS with many patches components. If docker or somebody else patches the kernel to m,ake it work somehow then I say ask for the source code to get these patches (the kernel is GPL after all so they have to provide them) and try to upstream them if that has not happened already.

The only thing we do control is how to configure the binfmt_misc mount to register the emulator in the kernel, if there is something we do wrong there you could patch that, https://github.com/containers/podman-machine-os/blob/613071a59c0e312cf0488c8404ea04903438b04c/podman-image/build_common.sh#L122-L123

I know that may not be what you want to hear but the truth is we simply have no resources to maintain totally custom builds, it is just FOCS with minor modifications from us.
Given this I don't see what we could do about it hence I am closing this.

@Luap99 thanks for you comment and explanation. 
I understand your position and it is how it is.

I'll investigate ways to solve it on our side.

Thanks! :)

> https://www.nocentino.com/posts/2026-02-02-sql-server-2025-cu1-fixes-avx-issue/

Tested and does not solve it for Podman.
